symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden tearing interscapular back pain radiating to abdomen/flank

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic descriptor and distribution for descending dissection.

symptoms.symptom_name: New cold, numb, weak left leg

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Confirms limb malperfusion from the dissection flap, a serious vascular complication.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poorly controlled hypertension and smoking history

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Both are major risk factors for aortic dissection.
Descending dissections often present with back/abdominal pain predominance rather than chest pain, unlike ascending dissection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Refractory hypertension (210/115) and diminished/absent left leg pulses

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objectively confirms both dissection and limb malperfusion; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tearing back/flank pain with new leg ischaemia and vascular risk factors together confirm descending aortic dissection with malperfusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency CT aortogram and urgent vascular surgical referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive diagnosis and assessment of limb viability and need for intervention.
Careful blood pressure control reduces ongoing aortic wall stress without compromising perfusion to the already-ischaemic limb.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Outpatient vascular ultrasound in coming days
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Would dangerously delay emergency treatment for a limb- and life-threatening condition.
